name: Real-Time Anomaly Detection
note: Estimates based on Azure retail pricing. Actual costs depend on usage, region, reserved instances, and enterprise agreements.

services.name: Azure Event Hubs
services.purpose: High-throughput stream ingestion — sensor data, logs, transactions at millions of events/sec

services.name: Azure Stream Analytics
services.purpose: Real-time windowed aggregations — sliding windows, tumbling windows, pattern detection

services.name: Azure OpenAI
services.purpose: Anomaly reasoning — LLM explains detected anomalies, generates root cause hypotheses

services.name: Cosmos DB
services.purpose: Anomaly event store — detected anomalies, context snapshots, investigation history

services.name: Azure Functions
services.purpose: Anomaly action orchestrator — triggers alerts, escalations, auto-remediation workflows

services.name: Application Insights
services.purpose: Pipeline observability — stream processing latency, anomaly detection accuracy, false positive rate

services.name: Key Vault
services.purpose: Event Hub connection strings, OpenAI API keys, webhook secrets for alert routing

services.name: Azure Notification Hubs
services.purpose: Multi-channel alert delivery — push notifications, email, SMS for anomaly alerts

optimization_tips: Use Stream Analytics auto-scaling — start with 1 SU and scale based on SU% utilization metric
Route only anomalies (not all events) to Azure OpenAI — reduces token costs by 95%+
Use Event Hubs capture to archive raw streams to Blob Storage at $0.02/GB for cold analysis
Use Cosmos DB TTL to auto-expire anomaly records after investigation window (7-30 days)
Batch anomaly summaries — call GPT-4o once per minute with grouped anomalies instead of per-event
Use Event Hubs Basic tier for dev — Standard only needed for multiple consumer groups
